title: TeraWulf Reports Second Quarter 2024 Financial Results
text: TeraWulf Q2 2024 revenue reached $35.6 million, representing a 130.2% year-over-year increase from $15.5 million in Q2 2023. Gross profit (excluding depreciation) grew to $21.7 million, up 109.4% from $10.3 million. Gross profit margin decreased to 60.9% from 66.9%, primarily due to approximate doubling in network difficulty and the bitcoin reward halving in April 2024. Non-GAAP Adjusted EBITDA reached $19.5 million in Q2 2024, a 156.4% increase from $7.6 million in Q2 2023. The company self-mined 539 bitcoin at Lake Mariner and 699 bitcoin across both facilities, representing a 21.4% decrease year-over-year. Operating hashrate capacity increased 80% to 8.8 EH/s. Power cost per bitcoin rose significantly to $22,954 in Q2 2024 from $6,688 in Q2 2023, reflecting increased network difficulty. Average bitcoin value increased 135.9% to $65,984. The company eliminated all debt, paying down $30.2 million in Q2 and $75.8 million in July 2024. Cash position increased to $104.1 million from $54.4 million at year-end 2023, and stockholders' equity strengthened to $386.2 million.
